title: Is right to vote a fundamental right? Supreme Court answers
description: The Supreme Court recently reiterated that the right to vote is not a fundamental right but a statutory right that exists only where provided by law.On April 10, a bench of Justices BV Nagarathna and R Mahadevan emphasised that the right to vote and the right to contest elections are distinct rights and are governed by statutory provisions (provisions laid down by law) rather than Constitutional guarantees.It held that these rights are not absolute but are subject to conditions prescri
